source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Consistent Naming Convention is used for Organizational AMI

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Consistent Naming Convention is used for Organizational AMI
input_row.description: The naming convention for AMI (Amazon Machine Images) should be documented and followed for any AMI's created.
input_row.rationale: The majority of AWS resources can be named and tagged. Most organizations have already created standardize naming conventions, and have existing rules in effect. They simply need to extend that for all AWS cloud resources to include Amazon Machine Images (AMI)
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine what AMI's are created: From the Console: 1. Login to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane, under Images, click AMIs. 3. Review the list of AMIs. 4. Confirm that the AMI Name matches the organizational image naming policy. From the Command Line: 1. Run aws ec2 describe-images. aws ec2 describe-images --owner self --region us-west-2 2. Review the list of AMIs. 3. Confirm that the AMI Name matches the organizational image naming policy. If any of the AMI Name's do not match the Organization policy refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: If the AMI Name for an AMI doesn't follow Organization policy Perform the following to copy and rename the AMI: From the Console: 1. Login to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane click Images, click AMIs. 3. Select the AMI that does not comply to the naming policy. 4. Click on Actions. 5. Click on Copy AMI. Destination region - Select the region the AMI is in. Name - `Enter the new Name` Description - `Enter the new description` Encryption - `Select` if it matches your image policy 6. Click on Copy AMI Once the AMI has finished copying. 7. Select the AMI that does not comply to the naming policy. 8. Click on Actions. 9. Click on Deregister References: 1. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ec2/describ e-images.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/WindowsGuide/AMIs.html

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires human judgment to determine if the AMI naming convention complies with the organization's policy. This type of check inherently involves subjective assessment and visual inspection, which cannot be automated without understanding the specific naming policy.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access EC2 Console:** 
   - Navigate to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/.
   - In the left panel, click on "AMIs" under the "Images" section.
   
2. **List and Review AMIs:**
   - Review the list of AMIs created under the account.
   - Compare each AMI's name to the organization's standardized naming policy.

3. **Verify Compliance:**
   - Ensure that the naming of each AMI complies with the established organizational naming conventions.
   - Take note of any AMIs that do not match the policy.

4. **Manual Verification via AWS CLI:**
   - Run the command: `aws ec2 describe-images --owner self`.
   - Examine the output to see the names of all AMIs.
   - Verify that each name adheres to the organizational policy.

5. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - All AMIs should have names that fully conform to the documented and agreed-upon naming conventions for the organization.

6. **Remediation:**
   - If any AMIs do not meet the standards, follow the remediation steps to copy and rename the AMIs accordingly, and then deregister the non-compliant AMI.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0

The nature of this check is oriented towards compliance and policy adherence, which inherently requires human oversight, especially since organizational naming conventions vary and might involve unique organizational identifiers, date formats, purpose descriptors, etc., which cannot be uniformly enforced or checked through automation alone.
